demo_mode_policy: No paid model call in demo mode. Demo serves pre-generated private fixtures only.
coupling_note: Configuration default only: the application keeps a provider/model abstraction with environment-based override and never hard-codes Replicate model ids in application logic.

excluded_models.schnell: Operationally disqualified before blind visual scoring: repeated Director E9828 runtime failures, two cells unresolved after the retry-2 limit; could not produce a complete balanced evaluation set.
excluded_models.klein-4b: Rejected for MVP use after blind scoring: lowest pooled mean (3.9333), four visual hard-failure flags including garment silhouette substitutions, a 502.9s latency outlier, and slightly higher observed screening cost than FLUX 1.1 Pro. Its 0.5s median-latency advantage does not compensate.

retained_challengers.flux-2-max: Retained as a research / potential future premium benchmark: highest-fidelity clean result (4.2833, zero hard failures) but ~5.9x slower by median latency and ~6.25x more expensive per screening generation than FLUX 1.1 Pro.
retained_challengers.flux-2-pro: Retained as a challenger, not the default: numerically highest pooled mean (4.2917) but one hard-failure flag, one transient first-attempt failure, ~2.4x slower by median latency and ~3x more expensive per configured screening attempt than FLUX 1.1 Pro.

decision_rationale: FLUX 1.1 Pro is the utility winner rather than the absolute numerical visual-score winner. Its pooled mean (4.2667) sits within 0.0250 of FLUX.2 Pro and 0.0166 of FLUX.2 Max - differences that are not meaningful in a single-rater, 12-brief screening - while it alone combines perfect 12/12 first-attempt reliability, zero hard failures, ~4.8s median latency, substantially lower cost ($0.040/generation observed), strong cultural coherence and strong bridal-occasion distinctiveness. Versus FLUX.2 Max it is ~5.9x faster and ~6.25x cheaper; versus FLUX.2 Pro it is ~2.4x faster and ~3x cheaper.
limitations: one visual evaluator (project owner), no inter-rater agreement
no real bridal customer or cultural-expert panel
decision is an MVP baseline and remains revisable as later stages produce evidence
open_questions: reference-image / inspiration influence (text-only vs metadata vs reference conditioning) — not yet evaluated; UNDECIDED
constrained refinement strategy (fresh regeneration vs image editing) — not yet evaluated; UNDECIDED
next_evaluation_stages: FLUX 1.1 Pro prompt-hardening for minimal/moderate embroidery briefs
rights-approved inspiration influence testing (text-only vs metadata vs reference image)
one constrained refinement test (fresh regeneration vs image editing)
privacy and image-retention verification against current provider terms
production integration with monitoring thresholds (latency, failure rate, spend)

evidence_manifest.note: This committed JSON is the CANONICAL machine-readable decision. Paths are repository-relative. Entries with committed=false are gitignored local evidence (large, private, or protected); when the evidence run is present locally, its artefacts must match the hashes below. The run-local model_decision.json is an evidence-run mirror, not the canonical decision. Aggregate hashes are SHA-256 over the files' bytes concatenated in sorted-filename order.

entries.description: Locked blind scoring sheet (single evaluator, 48 rows)
Reserve-before-spend budget ledger (68 settled entries, $5.76 spent of $10)
Canonical logical run state (55/5 first-attempt, 3 recovered, 2 unresolved)
PROTECTED blind-review mapping (kept out of the repository by policy)
Evidence-run mirror of this decision (must agree on all decision-critical fields when present)
